metadata.description: Held-out negotiation assertiveness pairs for probing only. NOT used in vector extraction. Same domain (negotiation) as extraction pairs but entirely new scenarios. Tests whether the assertiveness vector generalises within negotiation contexts.

dimensions.name: Assertiveness
dimensions.description: Making direct, confident requests and statements without aggression; stating needs and positions clearly

pairs.context: A client has not paid an invoice that is now 60 days overdue despite two polite reminder emails.
pairs.positive: I need payment of this invoice by this Friday. It is now 60 days overdue. If payment is not received by then, I will pause work on all active projects and issue a formal late payment notice.
pairs.negative: I'm sorry to keep chasing this — I know you're probably very busy. Whenever you get a chance to look at the invoice, that would be really appreciated. No rush if it's a bad time.

pairs.context: You are in a meeting where a colleague consistently interrupts you and talks over your points.
pairs.positive: I'd like to finish my point before we move on. I noticed I've been interrupted a few times and I want to make sure my perspective is heard.
pairs.negative: It's fine, I can share my thoughts another time. I don't want to hold things up. Let's hear what you were saying.

pairs.context: A contractor has submitted work that does not meet the agreed specifications and is asking for full payment.
pairs.positive: I'm not in a position to approve full payment for work that doesn't meet the agreed spec. I've documented the specific gaps and I need those addressed before we discuss final payment.
pairs.negative: I can see you've put in a lot of effort. There are a few areas that don't quite match what we agreed — maybe we could find a middle ground on the payment?

pairs.context: A landlord has failed to fix a heating issue for three weeks despite repeated requests and winter is arriving.
pairs.positive: I need this fixed by the end of this week. Three weeks without heat is not acceptable and it breaches the terms of my tenancy. If it is not resolved, I will contact the council's housing enforcement team.
pairs.negative: I know you're busy and I don't want to be a difficult tenant. I just wanted to mention again that the heating still isn't working. Please let me know when it might be sorted.

pairs.context: You have been assigned additional responsibilities at work without any discussion of increased pay or title change.
pairs.positive: I want to have a direct conversation about what has changed in my role. I've taken on significantly more responsibility and I think that needs to be reflected in my title and compensation. I'd like to schedule time to discuss this properly.
pairs.negative: I've sort of been doing extra things recently. I suppose at some point it might be worth a quick chat about it if there's ever a suitable moment.

pairs.context: A supplier keeps missing agreed deadlines and giving vague explanations.
pairs.positive: I need specific delivery dates in writing, not estimates. The pattern of missed deadlines is not acceptable and if the next deadline is missed, I will be invoking the penalty clause in our contract.
pairs.negative: I understand things have been complicated on your side. If there's anything I can do to make it easier for you to give me clearer timelines, just let me know.

pairs.context: A business partner is proposing terms that significantly favour them and brushes off your concerns.
pairs.positive: These terms don't work for me and I want to be direct about that. The profit split as proposed isn't balanced given the contributions each side is making. I need us to revisit this before we go any further.
pairs.negative: I suppose these terms could work, more or less. I have a few concerns but I don't want to hold things up. Let's just get something signed and we can revisit later if needed.

pairs.context: You are being asked to sign a contract with a clause that exposes you to unlimited liability.
pairs.positive: I won't sign this with unlimited liability. That clause needs to be capped at a defined amount. This is a firm condition, not a preference.
pairs.negative: I'm a bit uncomfortable with the liability wording but I don't want to seem difficult. Maybe it will be fine in practice.

pairs.context: A client keeps requesting changes after the scope was agreed and signed off, expecting them at no extra cost.
pairs.positive: These changes fall outside the agreed scope and they will be charged at our standard rate. I've been clear about this in our contract and I'm not able to absorb additional work without compensation.
pairs.negative: I'll try to fit the changes in. I want you to be happy with the result, so I'll just see how much time I have.

pairs.context: During a joint venture negotiation, your counterpart proposes a governance structure where you have no veto rights.
pairs.positive: I need veto rights on strategic decisions. That is not something I am willing to give up. If we can't agree on a governance structure that gives me that protection, I'm not in a position to proceed with this structure.
pairs.negative: A veto might cause friction. I suppose I could trust that things will work out and just monitor things carefully.

pairs.context: A recruiter calls to offer a role at a salary well below what you stated as your minimum.
pairs.positive: I stated my minimum clearly at the start of the process. The number you've come back with is significantly below that. If the salary can't reach my minimum, I'm afraid this won't be the right opportunity for me.
pairs.negative: That's a bit lower than I was hoping for. Maybe I could consider it depending on the other benefits. I don't want to close the door immediately.

pairs.context: A potential client has been non-committal for weeks and keeps asking for more information without making a decision.
pairs.positive: I need a decision by end of next week. I've provided everything you asked for and I have other clients I'm in discussions with. If I don't hear back with a clear answer by then, I'll assume you're not proceeding.
pairs.negative: I understand you need more time. I'm happy to send over anything else that might be helpful. Just let me know whenever you're ready.

pairs.context: Your employer asks you to work during a week you have booked as approved holiday.
pairs.positive: I've had this week approved and planned around. I won't be available to work during it. If there is a critical issue, we need to talk about alternative coverage, not about me cancelling approved leave.
pairs.negative: I suppose if it's really necessary I could check in here and there. I don't want to cause problems. What exactly would you need from me?

pairs.context: A partner in a deal is proposing a clause that lets them exit the agreement with only 14 days' notice.
pairs.positive: Fourteen days' notice is not workable for us. I need a minimum of 90 days to wind down operations and cover the exposure. That's a firm requirement from my side.
pairs.negative: Fourteen days is short but I suppose if we trust each other it probably won't come to that. I could probably manage in a pinch.

pairs.context: You have discovered that a client shared your proprietary pricing with a competitor.
pairs.positive: I need to address this directly. Our pricing is confidential and I have clear evidence it was shared with a competitor. This is a breach of our NDA and I need an explanation and a written commitment that it won't happen again.
pairs.negative: I'm not sure exactly what happened. It might have been a misunderstanding. I don't want to damage the relationship over something that may have been accidental.
